name: Semiconductors (SMH/SOX)
summary: Day 92 (Saturday May 30; Friday 29 May close). **THE AI-MEMORY SUPERCYCLE, FULLY DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS — AND THE DESTINATION OF THE CAPITAL LEAVING INDIA. KOSPI +3.6% to a record 8,476 (+28.1% MoM, +46.3% 3M); Taiwan TAIEX +2.5% (+27.5% 3M); SMH +19.9% MoM / +47.4% 3M. SK Hynix jumped ~11% to join the $1 trillion club (after Samsung and Micron) on HBM/high-end AI-memory demand; Goldman calls Korea its 'highest-conviction' market (KOSPI target 9,000; 2026 EPS growth +300%), Taiwan +45% EPS. **Critical concentration risk: 82% of Korean listed stocks FELL over the past month — Samsung + SK Hynix alone = ~50% of the KOSPI by market cap; this is the narrowest possible breadth, the index is two stocks. The won is WEAKENING (USD/KRW Won1,507, +4.8% 3M) even as KOSPI rips — FX outflow alongside equity inflow.** This is a structural AI-capex trade, ~0% crisis — it would run with or without the war. The node's standing flag that 'KOSPI is the most reversible position in the world if the AI/helium thesis cracks' stands, but NO crack is visible. New edge: nifty-50 <-> semiconductors (capital_substitution) formalizes that this is where EM capital leaving India goes (markets E1/C3).** Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **THE AI/HBM SUPER-CYCLE IS MELTING UP AND IS THE SINGLE LARGEST NON-WAR FORCE IN THE TAPE. SMH $601.53 (+1.0% 1D, +22.5% MoM, +48.0% 3M) — STRUCTURAL AI-INFRA DEMAND; KOSPI FRESH RECORD 8,228 (SK HYNIX JOINS SAMSUNG + MICRON IN THE $1T CLUB), TAIWAN TAIEX 44,256 (+11.7% MoM). PART OF THE BID IS CAPITAL ROTATION OUT OF EXPENSIVE OIL-EXPOSED INDIA INTO KOREA/TAIWAN AI (a NEW india↔semiconductor edge is being added by the lead engineer).** THE HELIUM PARADOX: the graph's documented cascade (Qatar/Ras Laffan → helium → semiconductors → tech valuations) would predict semis UNDER pressure — Qatar supplies ~1/3 of global helium and ~1/3 is offline. Instead semis are MELTING UP. WHY the cascade hasn't fired: South Korean chipmakers stockpiled ~6 months of helium, BUFFERING the disruption. This is a DELAYED cascade, not an ABSENT one — the non-confirmation (semis up despite helium offline) is INFORMATIVE: it DATES the cascade, it does not disprove it. Risk RE-EMERGES if the blockade runs past the stockpile horizon (~Nov 2026). Linde (helium proxy) calm = no near-term squeeze priced. The live vulnerability is AI-capex concentration (2 Korean names ≈ half of KOSPI), INDEPENDENT of the war. Do NOT model the AI rally as crisis-correlated (~95% non-war). PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 (Wednesday May 27 — ~48h window; no brief ran May 26). **THE AI/HBM SUPER-CYCLE IS GENUINELY DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS AND IS THE SINGLE LARGEST NON-WAR FORCE IN THE TAPE — it explains why US/Asia indices sit at records on a week of US strikes. SMH $592.59 (-1.6% 1D, +17.1% MoM, +45.8% 3M); the -1.6% today is a ONE-DAY BREATHER, not a regime change. The $1T-cap club is now Micron (May 26), SK Hynix (crossed Wed on +11%), and Samsung (earlier in May); KOSPI at all-time highs (~8,131, intraday 8,450), Taiwan TAIEX +11.8% MoM (TSMC). THE CRISIS CROSS-CHECK — the helium cascade is REAL on the supply side but BUFFERED: ~1/3 of global helium offline + spot +40-100% since the war (ACTIVE supply shock), YET Korean chipmakers stockpiled ~6 months of helium and large fabs are insulated by long-term contracts → the war→helium→semiconductor cascade is LAGGED ~6 months, NOT currently biting. Linde (helium proxy) is calm (+0.3% MoM), confirming no near-term squeeze priced. THE INTELLIGENCE: if the war runs past ~Q4 2026, the helium buffer empties and the cascade finally bites — exactly when the AI cycle is most extended. Watch the H2 'peak-out' warnings: concentration risk (2 Korean names ≈ half of KOSPI) is the vulnerability, INDEPENDENT of the war. Do NOT model the AI rally as crisis-correlated.** PRIOR (May 24): Day 86 (Sunday May 24 — markets closed). **SMH $576.32 FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE (+1.5% 1D, +19.6% MoM, +39.6% 3M). AI CAPEX CYCLE IS THE DOMINANT NON-WAR DRIVER OF 2026 — BIGGER MARKET FORCE THAN THE CRISIS, BUT QUIETLY RUNNING UNDERNEATH CRISIS HEADLINES. KOSPI +22.8% MoM / TAIWAN TAIEX +11.6% MoM / SMH +19.6% MoM / NIKKEI +8.3% MoM = COHERENT AI-CYCLE COVARIANCE PATTERN. ASIA TECH IS NOT A CRISIS TRADE. EVEN ON DEAL FAILURE, THE AI RALLY HAS ITS OWN LOGIC.** **Primary chain (~90% AI-cycle)**: Samsung+SK Hynix HBM cycle = 42% of KOSPI move. TSMC sympathy = Taiwan TAIEX +11.6% MoM. SoftBank/OpenAI IPO = single-stock Nikkei mover. **Secondary contributor (~10% crisis-relief)**: helium supply fear had spiked April → modest unwinding as deal hope returned; Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final qualification → June supply ramp positioned. **Investor implication**: Do NOT model these as crisis-correlated. **NEW PROPOSED EDGE (markets dossier E1)**: ai-capex-cycle as meta-regime — KOSPI/Taiwan/SMH/Nikkei cluster moves coherently on Nvidia/Samsung/SoftBank catalysts; 1M correlations ~0.8+. **Reversibility LOW for AI component**: multi-quarter regime, not daily event. Helium-mitigation thesis substantially validated by Samsung HBM4 June ramp. PRIOR (May 23): SMH $576.32 MAY 22 CLOSE — NVIDIA RESIDUAL LIFT + HELIUM-MITIGATION THESIS. NVIDIA RESIDUAL LIFT + HELIUM-MITIGATION THESIS (SAMSUNG HBM4 CLEARED NVIDIA + AMD FINAL TESTS) + AI-CYCLE REGIME IS THE DOMINANT NON-WAR DRIVER. KOSPI +22.8% 1M / TAIWAN TAIEX +11.6% 1M / NIKKEI +8.3% 1M — COHERENT AI-CYCLE COVARIANCE PATTERN.** **Primary chain (~75-90% AI-cycle)**: Nvidia Q1 (Feb-Apr) earnings released May 21: profit +200%+ YoY, revenue +85% YoY (CNBC, Invezz, IndexBox aggregator) → AI demand thesis affirmed at scale → chip-cycle bid revalidated; Samsung Electronics +7.5% May 22 after labor-union strike threat averted Wed late; SK Hynix +9.5% on HBM4/Nvidia partnership; TSMC +3% on May 22; Taiwan ex-China semi positioning bid. **Secondary contributor (~25-30% crisis-linked for SMH/KOSPI; ~10% for Taiwan)**: Helium-mitigation thesis — Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final qualification → June supply ramp positioned to handle helium-constrained Korean fab inventory window; 'helium crisis postponed but not solved' framing → near-term semi-supply continuity priced as positive; Korean fab inventory adequacy bid. **The cleanest non-war regime in the cycle.** Crisis has small ASYMMETRIC linkage to semis (via helium) but regime dominated by AI demand cycle. **1M moves are NOT crisis-driven** and would persist if war resolved tomorrow. They would also persist if war escalated, unless escalation directly hit Asian semi-supply (Hormuz strike closing Korean helium imports for >60 days would be the breaker). **Reversibility LOW** — AI-cycle is multi-quarter regime, not daily event. The 1M is now structural. **Crisis-divergence check**: USD/KRW ₩1,520.53 (+1.1% 1D, +2.7% MoM) DESPITE KOSPI surge = capital-direction divergence (equities bid, FX flows net outbound). Korean retail/institutional may be participating in equity rally; foreign capital is still net-outflow on macro-prudential grounds. **NEW PROPOSED EDGE: ai-cycle-regime as meta-regime node** — KOSPI/Taiwan TAIEX/Nikkei/SMH cluster moves COHERENTLY on Nvidia/Samsung/SoftBank catalysts; 1M correlations between SMH and KOSPI ~0.8+. This is a regime, not coincidence (Bloomberg 'Samsung TSMC-Led AI Stock Boom'; CNBC 'TSMC, Samsung, SK Hynix concentration risk'). PRIOR (May 22): **SMH $579.87 +2.1% 1D / +20.3% 1M / +40.4% 3M — NVIDIA Q1 EARNINGS BEAT + OPENAI IPO CHATTER + BOJ-HIKE EXPECTATIONS LIFT THE COMPLEX IN A REGIME DECOUPLED FROM THE CRISIS. HELIUM-MITIGATION THESIS (SAMSUNG HBM4 CLEARED NVIDIA + AMD) STRUCTURALLY CONSTRAINS KORNBLUTH 60-DAY CUT THESIS.** **Primary driver (~85% non-crisis)**: Nvidia Q1 earnings beat + AI-revenue trajectory + OpenAI IPO chatter (per IndexBox / TradingKey aggregator coverage) → Wall Street rally bid through Asia-Pacific session → Japan tech sympathy (Kioxia +3.7%, Fujikura +7.8%, Ibiden +7.7% per search); Taiwan TSMC AI-cycle leadership; chip-ex-China dominance. **Secondary chain (crisis-linked helpful, ~15%)**: US 10Y -0.5% 1D to 4.56% (yields easing into earnings risk-on); oil deal-anticipation removes inflation-cascade overhang; **Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final quality tests, June supply ramp** (Digitimes/Trendforce, intel A10); SK Hynix Q1 call disclosed 'sufficient helium inventory' + diversified suppliers (CNBC April 23) → helium-cascade Kornbluth thesis materially weakened — the 60-day production-cut risk channel closes. BOJ-rate-hike expectations on robust Japan export data (USD/JPY ¥159.14 effectively flat MoM). **The AI/semiconductor rally is structurally DECOUPLED from the crisis** — would have happened on Nvidia earnings regardless of Pakistan track. The crisis-link is asymmetric-helpful: deal-anticipation removes a marginal headwind, but the bull-leg's primary cause is the Nvidia/AI capex cycle. **Indian equity weakness is NOT entirely crisis-driven**: same yields-down + AI-cycle tape that bid Nikkei/Taiwan should bid Nifty too, and didn't (see nifty-50 node). **Nikkei 225 61,684.14 (+3.1% 1D, +3.6% 1M, +7.6% 3M)** — AI bid intact. **Taiwan TAIEX 41,368.21 (+3.4% 1D, +10.0% 1M, +22.5% 3M)** — AI-cycle leader; chip-supply-chain ex-China dominance. **KOSPI yfinance data hole; intel reports surged 'over 8%' per TradingKey aggregator (REPORTED single-source)** — operationally inconsistent with USD/KRW +1.2% (won weakening), needs verification. **Reversibility**: Crisis-component bid (~15%) reverses on Pakistan-track failure. AI-cycle bid (~85%) reverses on Nvidia guide-down or AI capex re-pricing — independent of the crisis. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **C3 SMH -3.8% / +22.3% 1M — TWO PRESSURES ARRIVING SIMULTANEOUSLY**: Primary chain Samsung-specific (~60%): Samsung Electronics 18-day strike scheduled to begin May 21 (3 days from today) following failure of all-night talks; Samsung stock plummeted 8.6% Friday with ~150 trillion won market-cap loss; could cost ~$20B. Micron -5% Friday to ~$735 on contagion fear; UBS semiconductors basket -4.2%; European tech -2.7%. 18-day production halt at Samsung could tighten HBM3/HBM3E inventories as AI server demand peaks; DRAM/NAND spot prices likely higher H2 2026. Secondary chain rates/macro (~30%): Friday April PPI +1.4% m/m → 10Y +14 bp to 4.59% → tech duration-trade compression; Warsh effective May 16 — markets pricing harder hawkish-balance-sheet reaction function. Tertiary chain helium/crisis (~10%): **Helium Day 3 of 14 TrendForce clock; KOSPI rallying Monday +0.6% contradicting kinetic-prep tape** (SK Hynix ₩1.836M +0.93%; 11 'emperor stocks' record). KOSPI +0.6% Monday after Friday's -6.1% = panic was priced Friday, not trend. **Assessment**: Friday -3.8% SMH is ~75% Samsung-strike-and-PPI-shock and only ~25% helium/crisis. **The crisis-component reverses ONLY if Korea memory ramp continues AND Samsung strike resolves AND Fed pauses** — none imminent. **SK Hynix is the relative-strength play within memory complex** (brokerages maintained ₩3.2M target prices). The 1M +22.3% SMH gain was vertical-AI-rally; the -3.8% is a real correction, not a regime change. **Reversibility**: Friday's panic likely already priced 18-day strike outcome; if Samsung-union resolution comes before May 21, SMH +2-3% snap. Helium tail-risk remains the structural overhang (Day 3 of 14). **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE PRICE IS FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSE: SMH $556.34 (-3.8% 1D, +22.3% 1M, +36.5% 3M).** Per markets dossier B: SMH -3.8% (Intel -6%, AMD -5.7%, Micron -6.6%, Nvidia -4.4%) on rates gap higher → multiple compression on long-duration tech + KOSPI sympathy + NVDA earnings positioning May 20. Crisis vs non-crisis: ~25% crisis / ~75% rates+earnings. **Samsung 18-day strike May 21 — 4 days from start, no new resolution development over the weekend; helium clock-expiry window now Day 2 of 14.** **Monday May 18 binary**: KOSPI gap and Samsung Monday open — strike-priced-in vs strike-not-yet-priced-in. **PRIOR (May 16):** SMH $556.34 -3.8% 1D / +22.3% 1M / +36.5% 3M — KOSPI knock-on (Samsung -8.6% + SK Hynix selloff) + pre-NVDA earnings (May 20) profit-take after vertical AI run + tech sold off hard on stagflation regime (Intel -6%, AMD -5.7%, Micron -6.6%, Nvidia -4.4%)**. **SAMSUNG 18-DAY STRIKE CONFIRMED MAY 21-JUNE 7** after talks at the National Labor Relations Commission office in Sejong collapsed after ~17 hours (Korea Herald May 13-14, Bloomberg, Tom's Hardware). Union demands removal of 50%-of-base bonus cap + 15% of annual operating profit for performance bonuses + structural contract formalisation; Samsung offered one-time 2026 payment but refused permanent changes. **At least 50,000 members expected to join. JPMorgan estimates potential losses up to ₩43 TRILLION ($28.8 BILLION) from a prolonged strike — MATERIALLY LARGER than the ~₩1T/day public anchor.** April one-day strike caused 18% drop in memory fab output and 58% drop in foundry output on affected shift. PM Kim Min-seok called emergency ministerial meeting. **HELIUM CLOCK-EXPIRY WINDOW MAY 16-23 BEGINS TODAY** (TrendForce 'two-week clock' from May 16 onward; ~8 days remain before Asian chip fab inventories exhaust at full production rates per March 24 TrendForce framework). The Samsung 18-day strike + helium clock-expiry + SK Hynix capacity-zero stack is now operationally synchronised. **NEW TRIGGER ADDED YESTERDAY** ('Samsung 18-day strike triggers KOSPI/SMH drawdown >5% within 5 sessions of May 21 start') is **partially pre-validated by Thursday's -6.1% KOSPI**; the JPMorgan ₩43T anchor is materially worse than the figures previously assumed. **Per markets dossier C3**: KOSPI/SMH triple-cause Korean crack: (a) Samsung strike confirmed ~40%; (b) Korea AI-Profit-Redistribution Proposal (Kim Yongbeom Facebook May 11-12 'AI national dividend') ~30%; (c) Foreign Capital Outflow Cascade ₩1.8T net sell May 15 alone, 7th consecutive session, cumulative ₩5.6T over 7 sessions, USD/KRW ₩1,497.76 broke 1,500 intraday ~30%. Iran rhetoric ~5%; helium tail risk structural not Friday-specific. **Phil Kornbluth (Kornbluth Helium Consulting)**: 'tsunami still a thousand miles offshore' framing held through Friday — but the helium-clock-expiry window May 16-23 BEGINS TODAY. Samsung 18-day strike is a separate-supply-side overlay that markets will conflate. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: JPMorgan ₩43T worst-case loss is structural; if strike resolves quickly OR Samsung resumes negotiations May 19-20, sharp bounce possible; if strike runs full 18 days, KOSPI another -10-15% downside. PRIOR (May 15): **C1 KOSPI -6.1% TO 7,493.20 — SINGLE LARGEST EQUITY MOVE OF CYCLE; SMH $578.34 (+1.0% 1D, +27.7% 1M, +41.9% 3M); Taiwan TAIEX 41,172.36 (-1.4% 1D, +12.1% 1M, +21.9% 3M); NIKKEI 61,409.29 (-2.0% 1D, +7.9% 1M, +8.1% 3M); NASDAQ Composite 26,635.22 (+0.9% 1D, +10.9% 1M, +18.0% 3M, RECORD).** **MAJOR: SK HYNIX AVAILABLE CAPACITY 'ESSENTIALLY ZERO'** per insider reporting; Alphabet/Meta/Microsoft/Nvidia/Amazon/Google offering unprecedented financing to fund dedicated production lines and ASML EUV lithography machines (hundreds of millions of dollars each). **Nvidia took the lion's share of 2026 output.** SK Hynix cautious about accepting customer financing because long-term supply agreements at discounted prices could crimp future profitability. Memory shortage now expected to last through 2027 or 2030. **SAMSUNG 18-DAY STRIKE PLANNED FROM MAY 21**: union demands removing the existing bonus cap, allocating 15% of operating profit to worker bonuses; April's one-day strike caused 18% drop in memory fab output and 58% drop in foundry output per shift. PM Kim Min-seok called emergency ministerial meeting. **HELIUM 2-WEEK-CLOCK at Day 74 since Ras Laffan halt March 2; helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23 begins tomorrow — most time-sensitive trigger in graph.** Combined: Korean fab complex now faces three simultaneous supply-side stressors (helium scarcity, 18-day strike from May 21, zero spare capacity). **NEW TRIGGER PROPOSED**: 'Samsung 18-day strike triggers KOSPI/SMH drawdown >5% within 5 sessions of May 21 start' — KOSPI -6.1% today is partial pre-validation. **Per markets dossier H**: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan most asymmetric DOWN trades on helium-shutdown materialization. Linde $511.65 (-0.3% 1D, +2.8% 1M, +6.5% 3M); BASF €53.39 (-1.1% 1D); industrial-gas bid INTACT structurally. — AI SUPER-CYCLE OVERWHELMING THE WAR**; KOSPI 7,941.95 (+1.2% 1D, +36.7% 1M, +39.9% 3M); Taiwan TAIEX 41,751.75 (+0.9% 1D, +15.0% 1M, +23.6% 3M); NIKKEI 62,654.05 (-1.0% 1D, +12.1% 1M, +10.3% 3M); NASDAQ Composite 26,402.34 (+1.2% 1D, +11.7% 1M, +16.9% 3M). **AI memory super-cycle: HBM4 demand from US hyperscalers + Chinese DC build-out + sovereign AI; Korea chip exports +150% YoY first 10 days May to $8.54B/10 days; JPMorgan raised KOSPI 12m bull-case to 10,000 (+26% upside); Goldman 9,000 'top conviction'.** **HELIUM 2-WEEK-CLOCK at Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt March 2; helium-clock-expiry window MAY 16-23 — most time-sensitive trigger in graph; if helium clock expires (mid-May → now), KOSPI is most exposed major index — would gut KOSPI/SMH +36.7%/+26.7% MoM rally.** Per markets dossier H: KOSPI/SMH/Taiwan are most asymmetric DOWN trades — pricing zero supply-chain risk while supply-chain risk is most quantifiable real-world risk in entire complex. SK hynix 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED, one-sided); no Samsung/SK hynix production-halt or cut announcement. Linde $513.26 (+1.9% 1D, +2.7% 1M, +6.8% 3M) industrial-gas bid INTACT. Per markets dossier C4: KOSPI/Taiwan/SMH rally is dominant global equity narrative — NOT a war-relief trade and would NOT reverse on war-resolution; would collapse violently on helium-shortage-forced-fab-shutdown (3rd-order Hormuz cascade market is not pricing). PRIOR (May 13): **SMH $576.31 (+1.7% 1D, +31.9% 1M, +41.9% 3M) — AI INFRASTRUCTURE DEMAND CONTINUES TO EXCEED HELIUM-CONSTRAINT IMPACT; Kornbluth 'tsunami a thousand miles offshore' framework intact.** **KOSPI -1.7% 1D (first profit-taking session of cycle after extraordinary vertical run) but still +33.1% 1M / +39.3% 3M;** TAIEX 41,898.32 (+0.3% 1D, +18.3% 1M, +24.1% 3M); NIKKEI 62,742.57 (+0.5% 1D, +17.4% 1M, +8.9% 3M); NASDAQ 26,274.13 (+0.1% 1D, +14.7% 1M, +16.3% 3M). **B9 HMM Namu forensics public May 11 — Seoul stopped short of publicly blaming Iran**, creating diplomatic friction with Trump (who pre-attributed). Forensic finding: 2 unidentified aerial objects ~1 min apart at the stern caused engine-room fire; mine + torpedo RULED OUT; engine debris recovered. First commercial-vessel strike of cycle with conclusive public forensic investigation. Korea Times: strike 'may shift Seoul stance' on potential South Korean involvement in any Hormuz protection mission. Korea-Iran-attribution friction with Trump = USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 (+1.9% 1D). **Helium tail-risk timeline tightening: Korean fab buffer now ~4 WEEKS REMAINING to mid-June exhaustion** (TrendForce framework). Tier-1 chipmaker production-halt falsification still HOLDS but window contracting. **AI-CapEx demand from Microsoft/Meta/Amazon unbroken — secular driver continues to dominate.** Linde $504.40 +2.3% 1D (industrial-gas pricing power on persistent helium shortage). For India deployment thesis: AI flows continue to bypass India IT services (Nifty IT -3% May 12 intraday despite weak rupee tailwind = US-growth fears dominating) and rotate to Korea/Taiwan/US tech.

recent_signals.headline: **AI/HBM MELT-UP CONTINUES — SMH $601.53 (+1.0% 1D, +22.5% MoM, +48.0% 3M). KOSPI FRESH RECORD 8,228 (SK HYNIX JOINS THE $1T CLUB), TAIEX 44,256 (+11.7% MoM). THE HELIUM PARADOX: SEMIS MELT UP DESPITE ~1/3 OF GLOBAL HELIUM OFFLINE — A DELAYED CASCADE, NOT AN ABSENT ONE.** (1) **Melt-up + structural demand**: SMH +1.0% to $601.53 (+48.0% 3M) on structural AI-infra demand; the AI lane explains why US/Asia indices sit at records on a week of US strikes inside Iran — KOSPI fresh record 8,228 (SK Hynix joins Samsung + Micron in the $1T club; Samsung + SK Hynix now >half of KOSPI cap), Taiwan TAIEX +11.7% MoM (TSMC). (2) **Capital rotation (new edge)**: part of the Korea/Taiwan AI bid is capital rotating OUT of expensive, oil-import-exposed India INTO Korea/Taiwan AI — the lead engineer is adding an india↔semiconductor capital-rotation edge. (3) **THE HELIUM PARADOX**: the graph's documented cascade (Qatar/Ras Laffan → helium → semiconductors → tech valuations) would predict semis UNDER pressure — Qatar supplies ~1/3 of global helium and ~1/3 is offline. Instead semis are melting up. WHY: South Korean chipmakers stockpiled ~6 months of helium, buffering the disruption. This is a DELAYED cascade, not an absent one — the non-confirmation is INFORMATIVE: it DATES the cascade (re-emergence horizon ~Nov 2026, when the stockpile empties), it does NOT disprove it. Linde (helium proxy) calm = no near-term squeeze priced. (4) **The vulnerability is concentration, not the war**: 2 Korean names ≈ half of KOSPI, independent of the crisis. **Do NOT model the AI rally as crisis-correlated (~95% non-war).**

recent_signals.headline: **AI/HBM SUPER-CYCLE DECOUPLED — THE SINGLE LARGEST NON-WAR FORCE IN THE TAPE. SMH $592.59 (-1.6% 1D = ONE-DAY BREATHER, +17.1% MoM, +45.8% 3M); MICRON/SK HYNIX/SAMSUNG EACH $1T. THE HELIUM CASCADE IS REAL ON SUPPLY BUT BUFFERED (~6-MO STOCKPILES) → LAGGED, NOT ACTIVE.** (1) **Decoupling**: the AI lane explains why US/Asia indices sit at records on a week of US strikes inside Iran — SMH +45.8% 3M, KOSPI all-time highs (~8,131, intraday 8,450, +90% YTD 2026), Taiwan TAIEX +11.8% MoM (TSMC). SK Hynix +11% Wed crossed $1T; Micron $1T May 26; Samsung $1T earlier in May → Samsung + SK Hynix ≈ HALF of KOSPI cap. SMH's -1.6% today is a one-day breather, NOT a regime change. (2) **The crisis cross-check — why the helium cascade is NOT biting**: the documented war→helium→semiconductor cascade is REAL on the supply side (~1/3 of global helium offline, spot +40-100% since the war) but BUFFERED — Korean chipmakers stockpiled ~6 months of helium and large fabs are insulated by long-term contracts → the cascade is LAGGED ~6 months, not active. Linde (helium proxy) calm at +0.3% MoM = no near-term squeeze priced. (3) **The forward intelligence**: if the war runs past ~Q4 2026, the helium buffer empties and the cascade finally bites — exactly when the AI cycle is most extended. (4) **The vulnerability is concentration, not the war**: watch the H2 'peak-out' warnings (Seoul Economic Daily) — 2 Korean names ≈ half of KOSPI, independent of the crisis. **Do NOT model the AI rally as crisis-correlated (~95% non-war).**
